facts.text: Michael Jackson's 'Thriller' album has sold over 70 million copies worldwide, making it the best-selling album of all time.
The Roland TR-808 drum machine, released in 1980, became one of the most influential instruments in pop music history despite being a commercial failure at launch.
Madonna is known as the 'Queen of Pop' and has sold over 300 million records worldwide throughout her career.
MTV launched on August 1, 1981, with the first music video played being 'Video Killed the Radio Star' by The Buggles.
The Yamaha DX7 synthesizer, released in 1983, became the best-selling synthesizer of all time and defined the sound of 1980s pop music.
BTS became the first K-pop act to top the Billboard Hot 100 with their 2020 single 'Dynamite'.
Prince could play 27 different instruments and played nearly all of them on his debut album 'For You'.
Taylor Swift's 'Eras Tour' became the highest-grossing concert tour in history, surpassing $1 billion in revenue.
The Moog synthesizer, invented by Robert Moog in 1964, revolutionized pop music by making electronic sounds accessible to mainstream artists.
Whitney Houston's rendition of 'I Will Always Love You' spent 14 weeks at number one on the Billboard Hot 100.
ABBA won the Eurovision Song Contest in 1974 with 'Waterloo,' launching their international career.
The Beatles hold the record for the most number-one hits on the Billboard Hot 100 with 20 chart-toppers.
Auto-Tune was invented by Andy Hildebrand in 1997, originally developed from technology used in seismic data interpretation for the oil industry.
Beyoncé surprised the world by dropping her self-titled visual album on December 13, 2013, with no prior announcement or promotion.
Max Martin has written or co-written more number-one hits than any songwriter in Billboard Hot 100 history except Paul McCartney and John Lennon.
The Roland Juno-106 synthesizer, released in 1984, became a staple of pop and synth-pop music with its warm analog sound and built-in chorus effect.
Lady Gaga's meat dress at the 2010 MTV VMAs became one of the most talked-about fashion moments in pop music history.
Mariah Carey holds the record for the most number-one singles by a solo artist on the Billboard Hot 100 with 19 chart-toppers.
The Spice Girls' debut single 'Wannabe' reached number one in 37 countries when it was released in 1996.
Ed Sheeran writes most of his songs using just a loop pedal and an acoustic guitar, layering sounds live to create full arrangements.
Michael Jackson's 'Thriller' music video, directed by John Landis, was the first music video to be inducted into the National Film Registry.
The LinnDrum, released in 1982, was the first drum machine to use digital samples of real drums and appeared on countless pop hits of the 1980s.
Elton John has had at least one song in the Billboard Hot 100 in every decade from the 1970s to the 2020s.
Stevie Wonder signed with Motown Records at age 11 and was billed as 'Little Stevie Wonder'.
The Backstreet Boys' 'Millennium' album sold over 1.13 million copies in its first week in the United States.
Billie Eilish recorded her debut album 'When We All Fall Asleep, Where Do We Go?' in a small bedroom studio with her brother Finneas.
Cher's 1998 hit 'Believe' was the first major commercial recording to use Auto-Tune as a deliberate vocal effect rather than a correction tool.
The TR-909 drum machine, successor to the TR-808, became essential in house and techno music and is still sampled in modern pop productions.
Adele's '21' album spent 24 weeks at number one on the Billboard 200, the longest run for any album since 1993.
K-pop trainees typically train for 2 to 7 years before debuting, practicing singing, dancing, acting, and foreign languages.
*NSYNC's 'No Strings Attached' sold 2.42 million copies in its first week in 2000, a record that stood for 15 years.
Quincy Jones produced Michael Jackson's three biggest albums: 'Off the Wall,' 'Thriller,' and 'Bad'.
Fleetwood Mac's 'Rumours' was recorded while the band members were going through painful breakups with each other.
The Fender Rhodes electric piano became a signature sound in pop ballads of the 1970s and 1980s.
Justin Timberlake's transition from *NSYNC to solo artist was marked by his debut album 'Justified' in 2002.
Taylor Swift was born on December 13, 1989, and considers 13 her lucky number.
BLACKPINK's 'DDU-DU DDU-DU' became the most-viewed music video by a K-pop group on YouTube.
Sampling technology in pop music took off in the 1980s with hardware samplers like the Fairlight CMI and E-mu Emulator.
George Michael's 'Faith' album sold over 25 million copies worldwide and won the Grammy for Album of the Year in 1989.
The Weeknd's 'Blinding Lights' became the first song to spend an entire year in the Billboard Hot 100's top 10.
Destiny's Child originally formed as Girl's Tyme in Houston, Texas, in 1990 before being renamed.
Phil Collins is one of only three recording artists who have sold over 100 million albums both as solo artists and as members of a band.
Dua Lipa's 'Levitating' was the longest-running Billboard Hot 100 top 10 hit by a female artist at the time of its chart run.
The Bee Gees wrote the Saturday Night Fever soundtrack in just one week, and it became one of the best-selling soundtracks of all time.
Pro Tools, introduced in 1991, transformed pop music production by bringing professional recording capabilities to digital platforms.
Ariana Grande is one of the few artists to have her first five studio albums debut at number one or two on the Billboard 200.
Michael Jackson's moonwalk was first performed on television during Motown 25 on March 25, 1983.
The Roland TB-303 bass synthesizer, intended as a bass guitar practice tool, accidentally spawned the acid house genre when musicians misused it creatively.
TLC's 'CrazySexyCool' is the best-selling album by an American girl group, with over 23 million copies sold worldwide.
New Kids on the Block were the first boy band to achieve massive commercial success in the modern era, paving the way for groups like Backstreet Boys and *NSYNC.
Britney Spears' '...Baby One More Time' was originally written for TLC, but they turned it down.
The Minimoog Model D, introduced in 1970, was the first portable synthesizer and helped bring synthesizer sounds into mainstream pop.
BTS's fan base, known as ARMY, has been recognized as one of the most powerful fan communities in the world.
Madonna's 'Blond Ambition World Tour' in 1990 is considered one of the greatest concert tours of all time.
Pharrell Williams produced or co-produced hits for artists including Britney Spears, Justin Timberlake, and Daft Punk as one half of The Neptunes.
Spotify launched on October 7, 2008, in Sweden, fundamentally changing how pop music is distributed and consumed.
The Oberheim OB-Xa synthesizer was prominently featured in Van Halen's 'Jump,' one of the biggest pop hits of 1984.
Whitney Houston is the only artist to have seven consecutive number-one singles on the Billboard Hot 100.
One Direction was formed on The X Factor in 2010 after each member auditioned as a solo artist and was eliminated.
Prince changed his name to an unpronounceable symbol in 1993 as a protest against his record label Warner Bros.
The electric bass guitar, popularized by Fender's Precision Bass in 1951, became the rhythmic backbone of virtually all pop music.
Lady Gaga's 'Bad Romance' music video was the first to reach 1 billion views on Vevo.
Adele's 'Hello' broke the record for most YouTube views in 24 hours when it premiered in October 2015 with 27.7 million views.
EXO debuted in 2012 as two sub-groups, EXO-K and EXO-M, promoting simultaneously in South Korea and China.
The vocoder, originally developed for military communications, became a popular vocal effect in pop music used by artists like Daft Punk and Bon Iver.
Mariah Carey's 'All I Want for Christmas Is You' took 25 years to reach number one on the Billboard Hot 100.
Max Martin's real name is Karl Martin Sandberg, and he is from Stockholm, Sweden.
The Beatles appeared on The Ed Sullivan Show on February 9, 1964, drawing an estimated 73 million viewers.
Taylor Swift's re-recording project ('Taylor's Version') was initiated to regain control of her original masters.
The Akai MPC (Music Production Center) revolutionized hip-hop and pop production by combining sampling with drum machine sequencing.
Beyoncé has won 32 Grammy Awards, the most by any artist in Grammy history.
The Spice Girls' debut album 'Spice' sold over 31 million copies worldwide.
Ableton Live, released in 2001, became a game-changer for pop music production and live performance.
Justin Timberlake's 'Cry Me a River' was rumored to be about his breakup with Britney Spears.
Billie Eilish became the youngest artist to win all four major Grammy categories in a single night at age 18.
The Bee Gees' Barry Gibb is the only surviving member of the trio, with Robin passing in 2012 and Maurice in 2003.
K-pop music videos frequently surpass 100 million views within their first 24 hours of release.
The clavinet, an electromechanical keyboard, gave Stevie Wonder's 'Superstition' its distinctive funky sound.
Ed Sheeran's 'Shape of You' was the first song to reach 3 billion streams on Spotify.
George Michael donated millions to charity anonymously throughout his career, a fact only revealed after his death.
Michael Jackson was born on August 29, 1958, in Gary, Indiana.
The Roland D-50 synthesizer introduced digital-analog hybrid synthesis in 1987 and was used extensively in late 1980s pop.
Ariana Grande's four-octave vocal range has often been compared to that of Mariah Carey.
TWICE became one of the best-selling K-pop girl groups of all time with sales exceeding 12 million albums.
Phil Collins' 'In the Air Tonight' is famous for its iconic drum fill, created using a gated reverb technique.
Madonna's 'Like a Virgin' was produced by Nile Rodgers, the legendary guitarist and producer from Chic.
The Super Bowl halftime show became a major pop culture event starting with Michael Jackson's performance in 1993.
Britney Spears' 'In the Zone' featured production from The Neptunes, Moby, and RedZone.
Drake became the first artist to surpass 50 billion streams on Spotify.
The Sequential Circuits Prophet-5, released in 1978, was the first fully programmable polyphonic synthesizer and influenced countless pop recordings.
Whitney Houston was born on August 9, 1963, in Newark, New Jersey.
BTS's 'Butter' stayed at number one on the Billboard Hot 100 for 10 consecutive weeks in 2021.
The Backstreet Boys have sold over 100 million records worldwide, making them the best-selling boy band of all time.
Dua Lipa studied at the Sylvia Young Theatre School in London before pursuing her music career.
ABBA's name is an acronym formed from the first letters of each member's first name: Agnetha, Björn, Benny, and Anni-Frid.
The talk box, a device that allows musicians to shape sound with their mouth, was popularized in pop by Peter Frampton and later by Roger Troutman.
Elton John's 'Candle in the Wind 1997,' dedicated to Princess Diana, is the best-selling physical single of all time with over 33 million copies sold.
Fleetwood Mac's 'Dreams' returned to the charts in 2020 after a TikTok video of Nathan Apodaca skateboarding and drinking cranberry juice went viral.
Lady Gaga was born Stefani Joanne Angelina Germanotta on March 28, 1986.
The Korg M1 workstation synthesizer, released in 1988, sold over 250,000 units and its 'M1 Piano' preset appeared on numerous pop hits.
Stevie Wonder released a string of classic albums from 1972 to 1976 often called his 'classic period,' including 'Innervisions' and 'Songs in the Key of Life'.
Lil Nas X's 'Old Town Road' spent 19 weeks at number one on the Billboard Hot 100, the longest run in chart history.
Destiny's Child launched the solo careers of Beyoncé Knowles, Kelly Rowland, and Michelle Williams.
The Weeknd's 'After Hours' album spawned the hit 'Blinding Lights,' which became one of the most successful pop singles of the 2020s.
Prince's 'Purple Rain' album spent 24 consecutive weeks at number one on the Billboard 200.
The Fender Stratocaster electric guitar has been a staple in pop music since the 1950s, used by artists from Buddy Holly to John Mayer.
BLACKPINK became the first K-pop girl group to perform at Coachella in 2019.
Taylor Swift is the first artist to occupy the entire top 10 of the Billboard Hot 100 simultaneously.
'We Are the World' was recorded on January 28, 1985, by a supergroup of 46 artists to raise funds for African famine relief.
Quincy Jones was one of the first African American composers to be widely accepted in the mainstream Hollywood film industry.
The wah-wah pedal, invented in 1966, became one of the most common guitar effects in pop and rock music.
Adele's '30' sold 839,000 copies on its first day in the United States, the largest single-day sales total since 2017.
TLC's 'Waterfalls' addressed social issues including HIV/AIDS and drug dealing, unusual themes for a pop single at the time.
Madonna performed at the Super Bowl XLVI halftime show in 2012, drawing an estimated 114 million viewers.
The concept of the 'album drop'—releasing music with no advance notice—was popularized by Beyoncé in 2013.
New Kids on the Block earned over $400 million in merchandise sales in 1990 alone.
The Boss DR-880, one of Roland's later drum machines, continued the legacy of programmable beats in pop production.
Ed Sheeran busked on the streets of London as a teenager before getting signed.
Mariah Carey wrote 'All I Want for Christmas Is You' in just 15 minutes on a Casio keyboard.
Michael Jackson's 'Bad' tour grossed $125 million, making it the highest-grossing tour at the time.
The Gibson Les Paul guitar became iconic in pop and rock after guitarists like Jimmy Page and Slash made it famous.
BTS performed at the United Nations General Assembly multiple times, speaking about self-love and youth issues.
Britney Spears' debut album '...Baby One More Time' has sold over 30 million copies worldwide.
The Fairlight CMI, introduced in 1979, was one of the first digital sampling instruments and cost over $25,000, yet was used by Peter Gabriel and Kate Bush.
One Direction became one of the best-selling boy bands of all time despite never winning The X Factor.
Ariana Grande's 'Thank U, Next' music video referenced four iconic rom-com films and broke YouTube's 24-hour viewing record.
George Michael's 'Careless Whisper' saxophone riff is one of the most recognizable melodic hooks in pop history.
ABBA: The Museum opened in Stockholm, Sweden, in 2013, celebrating the band's legacy.
The Mellotron, a tape-based keyboard instrument, gave The Beatles' 'Strawberry Fields Forever' its distinctive flute-like introduction.
Prince was born on June 7, 1958, in Minneapolis, Minnesota.
K-pop agencies like SM Entertainment, YG Entertainment, and JYP Entertainment have developed systematic training programs for aspiring pop stars.
Lady Gaga's 'Poker Face' was one of the best-selling singles of 2009, topping charts in nearly 20 countries.
The Weeknd funded a significant portion of his 'After Hours Til Dawn' tour's elaborate stage design out of pocket.
Phil Collins' drum sound on 'In the Air Tonight' was achieved by engineer Hugh Padgham accidentally leaving a talkback microphone on during recording.
The 12-string Rickenbacker guitar became synonymous with the 'jangly' pop sound of The Byrds and later influenced R.E.M. and Tom Petty.
Billie Eilish was born on December 18, 2001, and grew up in the Highland Park neighborhood of Los Angeles.
Dua Lipa's 'Future Nostalgia' album won the Grammy for Best Pop Vocal Album in 2021.
Elton John has performed over 4,000 concerts in more than 80 countries throughout his career.
The concept of streaming playlists like Spotify's 'Today's Top Hits' now rivals traditional radio in influence over pop music hits.
Fleetwood Mac's Lindsey Buckingham is known for his unique fingerpicking guitar technique, which he developed because he couldn't afford a pick as a teenager.
Madonna's 'Material Girl' video was inspired by Marilyn Monroe's 'Diamonds Are a Girl's Best Friend' performance.
The Leslie speaker cabinet, originally designed for Hammond organs, became a popular effect for guitars and keyboards in pop recordings.
EXO's 'The War' album sold over 1.6 million copies in its first month, setting records for K-pop album sales at the time.
Taylor Swift's '1989' album marked her official transition from country to pop music.
The tambourine is one of the most commonly used percussion instruments in pop recordings, often adding brightness to a track's rhythm.
Whitney Houston's 'The Bodyguard' soundtrack is the best-selling soundtrack album of all time.
Beyoncé's Coachella performance in 2018, dubbed 'Beychella,' was the first headlining performance by a Black woman at the festival.
Stevie Wonder was born Stevland Hardaway Judkins on May 13, 1950, in Saginaw, Michigan.
The Bee Gees have written or performed nine number-one singles on the Billboard Hot 100.
Auto-Tune's 'zero setting' creates the robotic vocal effect that T-Pain popularized in the late 2000s.
Justin Timberlake's Super Bowl XXXVIII halftime show in 2004 became one of the most controversial TV moments in history.
The acoustic guitar remains one of the most important instruments in pop music, used for both songwriting and performance.
BLACKPINK's Jennie became the first female K-pop solo artist to top the Billboard World Digital Song Sales chart.
Max Martin produced hits for artists ranging from Britney Spears to Taylor Swift to The Weeknd, spanning three decades of pop dominance.
Michael Jackson's 'Billie Jean' was the first video by a Black artist to receive heavy rotation on MTV.
The Wurlitzer electric piano, with its warm vibrato tone, was featured in classic pop recordings by artists like Ray Charles and Supertramp.
Adele won six Grammy Awards in 2012 for her album '21,' tying the record for most Grammys won in a single night at that time.
TLC's 'No Scrubs' topped the Billboard Hot 100 for four weeks in 1999.
Prince released his iconic 'Purple Rain' album on June 25, 1984.
The cowbell became an unexpectedly prominent percussion instrument in pop, famously featured in Blue Öyster Cult's 'Don't Fear the Reaper'.
BTS's RM and Suga both had underground rap careers before joining the group.
Mariah Carey has a five-octave vocal range, one of the widest ranges of any pop artist.
The Spice Girls are credited with popularizing the concept of 'Girl Power' in 1990s pop culture.
Dr. Luke (Lukasz Gottwald) produced numerous chart-topping pop hits in the late 2000s and 2010s for artists including Kesha, Katy Perry, and Kelly Clarkson.
Ed Sheeran's 'Divide' tour became the highest-grossing concert tour of all time before Taylor Swift's Eras Tour surpassed it.
The bass guitar provides the harmonic foundation in almost every pop song, bridging the rhythm section and the melody.
Lady Gaga's 'A Star Is Born' soundtrack featured 'Shallow,' which won the Academy Award for Best Original Song.
One Direction's 'Midnight Memories' was the best-selling album worldwide in 2013.
ABBA's music has sold over 385 million records worldwide, making them one of the best-selling music acts in history.
The Nord Lead synthesizer, introduced in 1995, brought virtual analog synthesis to pop stages with its distinctive red casing.
Britney Spears' conservatorship, which lasted from 2008 to 2021, sparked the #FreeBritney movement.
George Michael sold over 120 million records worldwide throughout his career as both a solo artist and as one half of Wham!

Beyoncé's 'Lemonade' was accompanied by a visual album that premiered exclusively on HBO.

Ariana Grande became the first artist since The Beatles to hold the top three spots simultaneously on the Billboard Hot 100.
The Backstreet Boys' 'I Want It That Way' has been voted one of the greatest pop songs of all time in numerous polls despite its famously nonsensical lyrics.

The synthesizer bass, played on keyboards instead of a traditional bass guitar, became a defining element of 1980s pop.

Stevie Wonder's 'Superstition' was originally written for Jeff Beck, but Motown executives insisted Wonder release it himself.

Taylor Swift won Album of the Year at the Grammys four times, the most by any artist.
Phil Collins was both the drummer and lead vocalist for Genesis before launching his hugely successful solo career.
The Weeknd spent $7 million of his own money on his 2021 Super Bowl halftime show performance.
TWICE's 'TT' music video was the fastest K-pop group music video to reach 100 million views at the time of its release.
